channel.title: Comments for page "Under Control"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Under Control"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is still some of the finest writing on the wiki.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Up voted for the appropriate use of lorem ipsum text for an incantation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Anyone from the Occult Studies from the Sloth's Pit hub would have fit in perfectly on this story.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: But, Deci, I can only upvote once.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hey, you people, you people should read this cause it's good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is really rather good - one of the first tales that has made Three Portlands feel *functional* to me. I look forward to more of Adam Rowe.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm not too familiar with Third Law stuff, but I enjoyed this nonetheless. (Except the red-to-white gradient with gray1 black text. That was painful. Perhaps a duller gradient, more of a white-to-reddish-gray? It looks like the red is pure #ff0000, so maybe something on the duller end, like #8d3939 or something.)

Footnotes
1. was in night mode when first looking at this

item.title: slow animals when
item.content:encoded:
lub u 2 ty <333

This is a fun little game of geas-and-mouse right here, and I appreciate all the little nods and chunks of worldbuilding you splice in throughout. Thoroughly enjoyable and exciting from front to back.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is a marvelous use of lore. I'm really impressed with how naturally everything comes together. It's all very clever and quite satisfying. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What a nice use of Seance Dust, and what a nice addition to the Third Law canon.

I really appreciate how your protagonist is able to out smart the Redzone goons, and feel like this is not the last we will see of them for a while.

+1. Twas a fun read.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This tale is dedicated to Taffeta, for whom it was created in the Forum Art Exchange.

Taffeta, I can't tell you how much I appreciate the support you give me, not just in writing, brainstorming, and critique, but also as a friend. I'm glad I had the opportunity to make this for you.

Adam Rowe's story continues in Comedown Machine.

CREDITS:

First of all, an extra-special thanks to my dear friend who critiqued, helped me brainstorm, and created the memetic image used in the piece. Please go appreciate him at his tumblr or his wordpress.

Second thanks go to GreenWolf and Conwell, for reviewing the piece.

Finally, thanks to the authors whose works I use in this piece — GreenWolf, Conwell, Taffeta, AJMansfield, and spikebrennan.

NOTES:

This tale is significant to me personally because, despite participating in the MTF Contest team that ended up creating Third Law, this is my first full contribution to the canon since the contest ended. It won't be the last.

P.S. gee taffeta! how come your mom lets you have two gift tales?
